subject: Astronomy & Space
title: Solar Flares
description: A short lesson to help you apply Solar Flares.

hook.text: Solar flares release energy equal to billions of nuclear bombs when magnetic field lines snap and reconnect.

buildup.text: Twisted magnetic loops on the Sun's surface reconnect violently, hurling radiation across space.

discovery.text: The 1859 Carrington Event was the strongest flare on record. Auroras appeared as far south as Cuba.

twist.text: A similar event today would fry satellites, crash power grids, and cause trillions in damage.

climax.text: NASA's Parker Solar Probe flies through the corona, gathering data to predict the next big eruption.

punchline.text: The Sun has a temper. Earth is always in range.

quiz.question: What was the Carrington Event?
options: The strongest recorded solar flare, causing auroras visible near the equator
The first time sunspots were observed through a telescope
A solar eclipse that lasted an unusually long time
